**#S1549** Updated ROADMAP.md with layered workspace architecture and graphify contribution strategy (Apr 10, 5:36 PM)

**Investigated**: The ROADMAP.md file structure and how to represent a multi-layered workspace rollout plan with trigger-based shipping conditions rather than timelines. Examined how to document the persistent workspace layers (0-3), graphify upstream contribution blockers, and the existing spec sync mechanism in a cohesive roadmap format.

**Learned**: Effective roadmaps for infrastructure work benefit from explicit trigger conditions ("ship when real users cache 5+ repos") rather than arbitrary timelines. The north-star principle "the workspace is an optimization, never a gate" governs all failure paths and should be stated upfront. Layered rollout strategies work best when each layer has clear preconditions and the entire system can degrade gracefully to simpler behavior.

**Completed**: ROADMAP.md has been restructured with three main sections: (1) Persistent Workspace with four layers (Layer 0 current, Layers 1-3 with specific trigger conditions for registry intelligence, tool tenants, and cross-repo intelligence), (2) Graphify Upstream Contribution with P0/P1 blockers and backup candidates, (3) Spec Sync Mechanism (preserved from original). The document now uses directional language instead of scheduled timelines and links back to detailed brainstorming docs. Changes are modified but not yet staged (branch dev is 8 commits ahead of origin).

**Next Steps**: Stage and commit the ROADMAP.md changes as part of the current work on the dev branch, which is already 8 commits ahead of origin and ready to be pushed.

Search: Use `query` with sub-queries (lex/vec/hyde):
  - type:'lex' — BM25 keyword search (exact terms, fast)
  - type:'vec' — semantic vector search (meaning-based)
  - type:'hyde' — hypothetical document (write what the answer looks like)

  Always provide `intent` on every search call to disambiguate and improve snippets.

Examples:
  Quick keyword lookup: [{type:'lex', query:'error handling'}]
  Semantic search: [{type:'vec', query:'how to handle errors gracefully'}]
  Best results: [{type:'lex', query:'error'}, {type:'vec', query:'error handling best practices'}]
  With intent: searches=[{type:'lex', query:'performance'}], intent='web page load times'

Retrieval:
  - `get` — single document by path or docid (#abc123). Supports line offset (`file.md:100`).
  - `multi_get` — batch retrieve by glob (`journals/2025-05*.md`) or comma-separated list.

Tips:
  - File paths in results are relative to their collection.
  - Use `minScore: 0.5` to filter low-confidence results.
  - Results include a `context` field describing the content type.

2 3 4 5 # Skill Forge (SKF) 6 7 **Turn code and docs into instructions AI agents can actually follow.** 8 9 [![Quality & Validation](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows/quality.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows/quality.yaml) 10 [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/bmad-module-skill-forge)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge) 11 [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) 12 [![BMad Module](https://img.shields.io/badge/BMad-module-blue)](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD) 13 [![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-%3E%3D3.10-blue?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org) 14 [![uv](https://img.shields.io/badge/uv-package%20manager-blueviolet?logo=uv)](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) 15 [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-online-green)](https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge/) 16 [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-Join%20Community-7289da?logo=discord&logoColor=white)](https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj) 17 [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge?style=social)](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/stargazers) 18 19 *Skill Forge analyzes your code repositories, documentation, and developer discourse to build verified instruction files for AI agents. Every instruction links back to where it came from — nothing is made up.* 20 21 **If SKF helps your agent stop hallucinating, give it a ⭐ — it helps others find this tool.** 22 23
24 25 --- 26 27 ## The Problem 28 29 You ask an AI agent to use a library. It invents function names that don't exist. It guesses parameter types. You paste documentation into the context — it still gets details wrong. You write instructions by hand — they go stale the moment the code changes. 30 31 This isn't an edge case. It's the default experience. 32 33 ## How Skill Forge Fixes This 34 35 1. **Analyzes your sources** — extracts real function signatures, types, and patterns from code repositories, documentation websites, and developer discourse 36 2. **Compiles verified instruction files** — every instruction links to the exact file and line it came from 37 3. **Version-aware** — skills are stored per-version, so updating to v2.0 doesn't break your v1.x skill. Compatible with [skill.sh](https://skill.sh) and [npx skills](https://www.npmjs.com/package/skills) 38 4. **Manageable lifecycle** — rename skills and drop deprecated versions without manual file surgery. Every instruction links back to where it came from — nothing is made up.* **If SKF helps your agent stop hallucinating, give it a ⭐ — it helps others find this tool.**
--- ## The Problem You ask an AI agent to use a library. It invents function names that don't exist. It guesses parameter types. You paste documentation into the context — it still gets details wrong. You write instructions by hand — they go stale the moment the code changes. This isn't an edge case. It's the default experience. ## How Skill Forge Fixes This 1. **Analyzes your sources** — extracts real function signatures, types, and patterns from code repositories, documentation websites, and developer discourse 2. **Compiles verified instruction files** — every instruction links to the exact file and line it came from 3. **Version-aware** — skills are stored per-version, so updating to v2.0 doesn't break your v1.x skill. Compatible with [skill.sh](https://skill.sh) and [npx skills](https://www.npmjs.com/package/skills) 4. **Manageable lifecycle** — rename skills and drop deprecated versions without manual file surgery. Transactional safety for destructive operations. 5. **Follows an open standard** — skills comply with the [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io) spec and work across Claude, Cursor, Copilot, and other AI agents ## Before vs After **Without SKF** — your agent guesses: ```python import cognee # Agent hallucinates: sync call, wrong parameter name, missing await results = cognee.search("What does Cognee do?", mode="graph") ```

# SKF Installer Banner Polish (v1.0 release prep)

## Context

SKF is shipping its first major release. The installer is the very first surface a new
user sees — it needs to feel finished, trustworthy, and point people at the right places
(docs, repo, community) without drifting into marketing bloat.

The current banner in `tools/cli/lib/ui.js` is already tasteful: amber/gold brand palette,
figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow inside a framed box, a one-line tagline. What it's missing
compared to a polished installer like `npx bmad-method install`:

- The tagline doesn't match the official README pitch ("Turn code and docs into
  instructions AI agents can actually follow").
- No pointers to docs, GitHub, or Discord — a new user has no "what next" footer.
- The frame is narrow (w=54), which makes the content feel cramped against the logo.
- No license/freeness signal — BMAD's "100% free, 100% open source" line builds trust.

BMAD's installer also has a *lot* we should NOT copy: corporate sponsorship pitch, donate
button, Twitter/Facebook/YouTube links, "Build More, Architect Dreams" marketing copy.
SKF's voice stays engineering-first.

Goal: tasteful polish, not a rewrite. Keep the existing structure and color system; change
only content, widths, and add a small resources block.

## Scope

Single-file change: `tools/cli/lib/ui.js`. No behavioral changes, no new dependencies
(figlet + chalk + @clack/prompts are already installed). The call chain from
`tools/cli/commands/install.js:12` → `ui.promptInstall()` → `displayBanner()` stays intact.

## Design

### Keep (unchanged)

- `displayBanner()` still called from `promptInstall()` at `ui.js:88`.
- Brand palette at `ui.js:25-30` (amber/gold/dark/spark) — already derived from
  `website/public/img/skf-logo.svg` hex values.
- Figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow font (~24 cols wide) — it's iconic, fits narrow terminals,
  and carries the brand.
- `intro(brand.amber('Skill Forge Installer'))` closing line so the @clack vertical flow
  starts cleanly after the banner.
- `displaySuccess()` end-of-install `note()` — it's already good.

### Change

1. **Frame width**: `w = 54` → `w = 68`. Total rendered width becomes 72 cols, still
   well inside an 80-col terminal, gives the logo and tagline room to breathe.

2. **Tagline content** (inside the frame): replace the current two lines
   (`Agent Skill Compiler` + `Code · Docs · Discourse → Verified agent skills`) with:
   - Line 1: `Skill Forge` bold + `⚒` spark  +  `Agent Skill Compiler` dim (combined
     on one row — saves vertical space vs. the current stacked layout)
   - Line 2: official README tagline: `Turn code and docs into instructions AI agents
     can actually follow.`

3. **Meta strip** (inside the frame, dim): `v{version}  ·  MIT License  ·  Open Source`.
   Version pulled from the existing `packageJson.version` read at `ui.js:34-35`.

4. **Resources block** (new, below the frame, before `intro()`): three aligned rows,
   printed with plain `console.log` (not `note()` — we don't want nested @clack framing).

   ```
     Docs      https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge
     GitHub    https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
     Discord   https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj
   ```

   Labels in `chalk.dim`, URLs in `brand.amber`. All three URLs are already verified:
   docs URL lives in `website/astro.config.mjs`, GitHub repo in `package.json:26`,
   Discord badge in `README.md:16`.

5. **Section separators** (BMAD-style but restrained): two `━` rules in `brand.dark`,
   one below the frame and one below the resources block, to visually bracket the
   "welcome" zone from the prompts that follow.

### Deliberately NOT doing

- No "star us" separate pitch — the GitHub line is enough.
- No donate/sponsor link — SKF has no funding config and shouldn't invent one.
- No Twitter/X/YouTube/Facebook links — SKF doesn't have that surface.
- No dynamic terminal-width detection (`process.stdout.columns`) — current code uses a
  fixed width and 72 cols is safe for 80-col terminals, which is the de-facto floor.
- No change to `displaySuccess()` — it already shows the docs URL in `outro()` at
  `ui.js:323-325` and the framed "Installation complete" note.
- No change to `promptInstall()` prompt flow (lines 87-249) — only `displayBanner()`
  is being touched.
- No new dependencies.

## Rendered preview

```

  ║                                                                    ║

  ║    ██╔════╝██║ ██╔╝██╔════╝                                        ║
  ║    ███████╗█████╔╝ █████╗                                          ║
  ║    ╚════██║██╔═██╗ ██╔══╝                                          ║
  ║    ███████║██║  ██╗██║                                             ║
  ║    ╚══════╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝                                             ║
  ║                                                                    ║
  ╟────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╢
  ║  Skill Forge  ⚒  Agent Skill Compiler                              ║
  ║  Turn code and docs into instructions AI agents can follow.        ║
  ║  v0.10.0  ·  MIT License  ·  Open Source                           ║
  ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  Docs      https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge
  GitHub    https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
  Discord   https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj

  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

◇  Skill Forge Installer
│
◇  Target: /home/user/project
…
```

## Files modified

- `tools/cli/lib/ui.js` — only the `displayBanner()` method (lines 33-72) and the
  constant `w = 54`. Every other method stays untouched.

## Verification

1. **Visual smoke test** — run the installer in a scratch dir and eyeball the banner:
   ```
   mkdir -p /tmp/skf-banner-test && cd /tmp/skf-banner-test
   node /home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/tools/cli/skf-cli.js install
   ```
   Cancel at the first prompt (Ctrl+C). Confirm:
   - Logo is centered in frame, no ragged right edge.
   - All three URLs render in full without wrapping.
   - Colors resolve (amber logo, dim labels, amber URLs, dark frame).
   - No extra blank lines between frame and `intro()`.

2. **Narrow terminal test** — resize terminal to exactly 80 cols and re-run; frame
   should still fit without wrapping.

3. **Existing test suite** — `npm run test:install` exercises the installer
   (`test/test-installation-components.js`). Run it to confirm nothing regressed.

4. **Update flow** — run the installer a second time in the same dir to hit the
   "existing installation detected" branch (`ui.js:99-138`). The banner should render
   identically; only the prompt that follows differs.

5. **Lint + format** — `npm run lint` and `npm run format:check` to catch style drift.

# SKF Installer Banner Polish (v1.0 release prep)

## Context

SKF is shipping its first major release. The installer is the very first surface a new
user sees — it needs to feel finished, trustworthy, and point people at the right places
(docs, repo, community) without drifting into marketing bloat.

The current banner in `tools/cli/lib/ui.js` is already tasteful: amber/gold brand palette,
figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow inside a framed box, a one-line tagline. What it's missing
compared to a polished installer like `npx bmad-method install`:

- The tagline doesn't match the official README pitch ("Turn code and docs into
  instructions AI agents can actually follow").
- No pointers to docs, GitHub, or Discord — a new user has no "what next" footer.
- The frame is narrow (w=54), which makes the content feel cramped against the logo.
- No license/freeness signal — BMAD's "100% free, 100% open source" line builds trust.

BMAD's installer also has a *lot* we should NOT copy: corporate sponsorship pitch, donate
button, Twitter/Facebook/YouTube links, "Build More, Architect Dreams" marketing copy.
SKF's voice stays engineering-first.

Goal: tasteful polish, not a rewrite. Keep the existing structure and color system; change
only content, widths, and add a small resources block.

## Scope

Single-file change: `tools/cli/lib/ui.js`. No behavioral changes, no new dependencies
(figlet + chalk + @clack/prompts are already installed). The call chain from
`tools/cli/commands/install.js:12` → `ui.promptInstall()` → `displayBanner()` stays intact.

## Design

### Keep (unchanged)

- `displayBanner()` still called from `promptInstall()` at `ui.js:88`.
- Brand palette at `ui.js:25-30` (amber/gold/dark/spark) — already derived from
  `website/public/img/skf-logo.svg` hex values.
- Figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow font (~24 cols wide) — it's iconic, fits narrow terminals,
  and carries the brand.
- `intro(brand.amber('Skill Forge Installer'))` closing line so the @clack vertical flow
  starts cleanly after the banner.
- `displaySuccess()` end-of-install `note()` — it's already good.

### Change

1. **Frame width**: `w = 54` → `w = 68`. Total rendered width becomes 72 cols, still
   well inside an 80-col terminal, gives the logo and tagline room to breathe.

2. **Tagline content** (inside the frame): replace the current two lines
   (`Agent Skill Compiler` + `Code · Docs · Discourse → Verified agent skills`) with:
   - Line 1: `Skill Forge` bold + `⚒` spark  +  `Agent Skill Compiler` dim (combined
     on one row — saves vertical space vs. the current stacked layout)
   - Line 2: official README tagline: `Turn code and docs into instructions AI agents
     can actually follow.`

3. **Meta strip** (inside the frame, dim): `v{version}  ·  MIT License  ·  Open Source`.
   Version pulled from the existing `packageJson.version` read at `ui.js:34-35`.

4. **Resources block** (new, below the frame, before `intro()`): three aligned rows,
   printed with plain `console.log` (not `note()` — we don't want nested @clack framing).

   ```
     Docs      https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge
     GitHub    https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
     Discord   https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj
   ```

   Labels in `chalk.dim`, URLs in `brand.amber`. All three URLs are already verified:
   docs URL lives in `website/astro.config.mjs`, GitHub repo in `package.json:26`,
   Discord badge in `README.md:16`.

5. **Section separators** (BMAD-style but restrained): two `━` rules in `brand.dark`,
   one below the frame and one below the resources block, to visually bracket the
   "welcome" zone from the prompts that follow.

### Deliberately NOT doing

- No "star us" separate pitch — the GitHub line is enough.
- No donate/sponsor link — SKF has no funding config and shouldn't invent one.
- No Twitter/X/YouTube/Facebook links — SKF doesn't have that surface.
- No dynamic terminal-width detection (`process.stdout.columns`) — current code uses a
  fixed width and 72 cols is safe for 80-col terminals, which is the de-facto floor.
- No change to `displaySuccess()` — it already shows the docs URL in `outro()` at
  `ui.js:323-325` and the framed "Installation complete" note.
- No change to `promptInstall()` prompt flow (lines 87-249) — only `displayBanner()`
  is being touched.
- No new dependencies.

# SKF Installer Banner Polish (v1.0 release prep)

## Context

SKF is shipping its first major release. The installer is the very first surface a new
user sees — it needs to feel finished, trustworthy, and point people at the right places
(docs, repo, community) without drifting into marketing bloat.

The current banner in `tools/cli/lib/ui.js` is already tasteful: amber/gold brand palette,
figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow inside a framed box, a one-line tagline. What it's missing
compared to a polished installer like `npx bmad-method install`:

- The tagline doesn't match the official README pitch ("Turn code and docs into
  instructions AI agents can actually follow").
- No pointers to docs, GitHub, or Discord — a new user has no "what next" footer.
- The frame is narrow (w=54), which makes the content feel cramped against the logo.
- No license/freeness signal — BMAD's "100% free, 100% open source" line builds trust.

BMAD's installer also has a *lot* we should NOT copy: corporate sponsorship pitch, donate
button, Twitter/Facebook/YouTube links, "Build More, Architect Dreams" marketing copy.
SKF's voice stays engineering-first.

Goal: tasteful polish, not a rewrite. Keep the existing structure and color system; change
only content, widths, and add a small resources block.

## Scope

Single-file change: `tools/cli/lib/ui.js`. No behavioral changes, no new dependencies
(figlet + chalk + @clack/prompts are already installed). The call chain from
`tools/cli/commands/install.js:12` → `ui.promptInstall()` → `displayBanner()` stays intact.

## Design

### Keep (unchanged)

- `displayBanner()` still called from `promptInstall()` at `ui.js:88`.
- Brand palette at `ui.js:25-30` (amber/gold/dark/spark) — already derived from
  `website/public/img/skf-logo.svg` hex values.
- Figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow font (~24 cols wide) — it's iconic, fits narrow terminals,
  and carries the brand.
- `intro(brand.amber('Skill Forge Installer'))` closing line so the @clack vertical flow
  starts cleanly after the banner.
- `displaySuccess()` end-of-install `note()` — it's already good.

### Change

1. **Frame width**: `w = 54` → `w = 68`. Total rendered width becomes 72 cols, still
   well inside an 80-col terminal, gives the logo and tagline room to breathe.

2. **Tagline content** (inside the frame): replace the current two lines
   (`Agent Skill Compiler` + `Code · Docs · Discourse → Verified agent skills`) with:
   - Line 1: `Skill Forge` bold + `⚒` spark  +  `Agent Skill Compiler` dim (combined
     on one row — saves vertical space vs. the current stacked layout)
   - Line 2: official README tagline: `Turn code and docs into instructions AI agents
     can actually follow.`

3. **Meta strip** (inside the frame, dim): `v{version}  ·  MIT License  ·  Open Source`.
   Version pulled from the existing `packageJson.version` read at `ui.js:34-35`.

4. **Resources block** (new, below the frame, before `intro()`): four aligned rows,
   printed with plain `console.log` (not `note()` — we don't want nested @clack framing).

   ```
     Docs      https://armelhbobdad.github.io/bmad-module-skill-forge
     GitHub    https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
     Discord   https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj
   ```
   ```
     Support   https://buymeacoffee.com/armelhbobdad
   ```

   Labels in `chalk.dim`, URLs in `brand.amber`. URLs verified:
   - Docs → `website/astro.config.mjs`
   - GitHub → `package.json:26`
   - Discord → `README.md:16`
   - Support → Buy Me a Coffee link provided by maintainer (not yet in repo / FUNDING.yml).

5. **Section separators** (BMAD-style but restrained): two `━` rules in `brand.dark`,
   one below the frame and one below the resources block, to visually bracket the
   "welcome" zone from the prompts that follow.

### Deliberately NOT doing

- No "star us" separate pitch — the GitHub line is enough.
- No Twitter/X/YouTube/Facebook links — SKF doesn't have that surface.
- No dynamic terminal-width detection (`process.stdout.columns`) — current code uses a
  fixed width and 72 cols is safe for 80-col terminals, which is the de-facto floor.
- No change to `displaySuccess()` — it already shows the docs URL in `outro()` at
  `ui.js:323-325` and the framed "Installation complete" note.
- No change to `promptInstall()` prompt flow (lines 87-249) — only `displayBanner()`
  is being touched.
- No new dependencies.

## Files modified

## Verification

1. **Visual smoke test** — run the installer in a scratch dir and eyeball the banner:
   ```
   mkdir -p /tmp/skf-banner-test && cd /tmp/skf-banner-test
   ```

2. **Narrow terminal test** — resize terminal to exactly 80 cols and re-run; frame

# SKF Installer Banner Polish (v1.0 release prep)

## Context

SKF is shipping its first major release. The installer is the very first surface a new
user sees — it needs to feel finished, trustworthy, and point people at the right places
(docs, repo, community) without drifting into marketing bloat.

The current banner in `tools/cli/lib/ui.js` is already tasteful: amber/gold brand palette,
figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow inside a framed box, a one-line tagline. What it's missing
compared to a polished installer like `npx bmad-method install`:

- The tagline doesn't match the official README pitch ("Turn code and docs into
  instructions AI agents can actually follow").
- No pointers to docs, GitHub, or Discord — a new user has no "what next" footer.
- The frame is narrow (w=54), which makes the content feel cramped against the logo.
- No license/freeness signal — BMAD's "100% free, 100% open source" line builds trust.

BMAD's installer also has a *lot* we should NOT copy: corporate sponsorship pitch, donate
button, Twitter/Facebook/YouTube links, "Build More, Architect Dreams" marketing copy.
SKF's voice stays engineering-first.

Goal: tasteful polish, not a rewrite. Keep the existing structure and color system; change
only content, widths, and add a small resources block.

## Scope

Single-file change: `tools/cli/lib/ui.js`. No behavioral changes, no new dependencies
(figlet + chalk + @clack/prompts are already installed). The call chain from
`tools/cli/commands/install.js:12` → `ui.promptInstall()` → `displayBanner()` stays intact.

## Design

### Keep (unchanged)

- `displayBanner()` still called from `promptInstall()` at `ui.js:88`.
- Brand palette at `ui.js:25-30` (amber/gold/dark/spark) — already derived from
  `website/public/img/skf-logo.svg` hex values.
- Figlet "SKF" in ANSI Shadow font (~24 cols wide) — it's iconic, fits narrow terminals,
  and carries the brand.
- `intro(brand.amber('Skill Forge Installer'))` closing line so the @clack vertical flow
  starts cleanly after the banner.
- `displaySuccess()` end-of-install `note()` — it's already good.

### Change

1. **Frame width**: `w = 54` → `w = 68`. Total rendered width becomes 72 cols, still
   well inside an 80-col terminal, gives the logo and tagline room to breathe.

2. **Tagline content** (inside the frame): replace the current two lines
   (`Agent Skill Compiler` + `Code · Docs · Discourse → Verified agent skills`) with:
   - Line 1: `Skill Forge` bold + `⚒` spark  +  `Agent Skill Compiler` dim (combined
     on one row — saves vertical space vs. the current stacked layout)

     can actually follow.`
